description: We observed the empidonax flycatcher at DePoorter Lake near Julesburg.  As we walked down the fenceline, the bird moved along ahead of us and perched repeatedly on the fence.  Some of the characteristics we observed were olive-colored back; grayish face; narrow, complete eyering; thick, longish bill; and longish primary extension.  The bird called "peep" twice, reminiscent of a single Olive-sided Flycatcher call note (as described by Sibley).
